item.title: Flood Re Set to ‘Layer Up’ on Cat Bonds Amid Hedge Fund Demand
item.description: Britain’s ultimate backstop against flood-related losses is planning to rely more on the capital markets, after its first ever catastrophe bond drew a lot of interest from specialist investors including hedge fund Fermat Capital Management.

item.title: Africa's Critical Minerals Moment | Bloomberg Next Africa
item.description: In this episode of Next Africa, we examine the intensifying global race for critical minerals and how the continent can turn surging demand into lasting economic gains. One country looking to seize the opportunity is Botswana. Long known for its diamonds, the nation is now pivoting toward a more diversified minerals future. Bloomberg's Jennifer Zabasajja speaks with Minerals and Energy Minister Bogolo Kenewendo on what comes next and how Botswana plans to capture more value from its resources. Plus, from cobalt and copper to cutting-edge artificial intelligence, we explore how mineral exploration is being reshaped and why KoBold Metals is launching the largest exploration campaign of any company operating in the Democratic Republic of Congo. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Deutscher Rüstungsboom
item.description: Die Aufrüstung der Bundeswehr beflügelt die deutsche Wirtschaft mehr als viele erwartet hatten. Die Investitionen könnten möglicherweise sogar wichtiger sein als das Infrastrukturpaket. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Lithium Producer SQM Sees Global Demand Growing 25% This Year
item.description: Top lithium producer SQM said it sees world demand for the battery metal growing about 25% this year, after reporting record quarterly sales volume as the industry emerges from a global glut.

item.title: Pakistan, Afghanistan Threaten War in Region Eyed by Superpowers
item.description: Pakistan and Afghanistan edged closer to all-out war this week, escalating tensions in a region where the world’s economic superpowers are vying for influence.

item.title: China Fundraising Jumps as Refinancing and Bond Issuance Boom
item.description: China’s capital markets rebounded last year, fueled by a surge in equity refinancing and bond issuance, according to the National Bureau of Statistics.

item.title: CP Group to Sell 10% Stake in True Corp. With UBS as Main Buyer
item.description: Thai business conglomerate Charoen Pokphand Group plans to sell a 10% stake in its telecommunications affiliate True Corp., with most of the shares going to UBS Group AG.

item.title: Stocks Slide as Credit Stress, War and AI Fears Weigh | The Close 2/27/2026
item.description: Bloomberg Television brings you the latest news and analysis leading up to the final minutes and seconds before and after the closing bell on Wall Street. Today's guests are Robinhood Markets’ Stephanie Guild, Generate:Biomedicines’ Mike Nally, S&P Global’s Simon Gallagher, Inflation Insights’ Omair Sharif, Lightshed Partners’ Rich Greenfield, Fidelity’s Jurrien Timmer, Institute for Advance Study’s Alondra Nelson, Hornets Sports and Entertainment’s Shelly Cayette-Weston, Raising Cane’s Aj Kumaran, and Carnegie Endowment for International Peace Senior Fellow Aaron David Miller. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Ex-Moelis Banker to Plead Guilty in Global Insider Trading Case
item.description: A former Moelis & Co. investment banker plans to return to the US from France to plead guilty to tipping off members of a global insider-trading ring.

item.title: President Trump’s Economy: Boom, Bust, or Business as Usual?
item.description: President Trump says inflation is plunging, incomes are rising, and the economy is “roaring.” Former Obama economic adviser Jason Furman offers a reality check. Growth has been steady and unemployment stable, but tariffs are adding costs, policy uncertainty remains high, and the AI boom may not yet justify massive investment. With the Supreme Court striking down key tariffs and refund battles looming, the bigger question is whether today’s stability masks deeper long-term risks. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: US and Israel launch ‘massive’ strikes on Iran as Trump calls for regime change
item.description: US president urges Iranians to ‘take over your government’

item.title: Defensive munition shortages to shape attack on Iran
item.description: US and Israel burned through interceptors at an unprecedented rate during last year’s 12-day war

item.title: Pete Hegseth, Maga’s man at the Pentagon
item.description: Trump’s defence secretary is on the front lines of the culture wars — but can he lead the US in a battle against Iran?

item.title: Key areas of contention between Iran and the US
item.description: Washington has raised concerns about Tehran’s nuclear programme, ballistic missiles and allied militant groups

item.title: Anthropic to sue Trump administration after AI lab is labelled security risk
item.description: Pentagon bans start-up from government contracts, as OpenAI announces deal to deploy models in classified networks

item.title: DeepSeek to release long-awaited AI model in new challenge to US rivals
item.description: Chinese AI group has worked with Huawei to cut reliance on Nvidia chips

item.title: Project Warrior: How Paramount beat Netflix in $110bn battle for Warner
item.description: Ellison father and son viewed deal as a matter of survival to compete with tech giants that now dominate Hollywood

item.title: Collapse of UK property lender sends shockwaves through Wall Street
item.description: Lenders weigh losses linked to Market Financial Solutions, which financed a Bangladeshi politician’s real estate empire

item.title: US bank stocks record biggest slide since April’s market ructions
item.description: Investors fret over growing strains in private credit and risks of AI disrupting the software industry

item.title: Impounded Russia-linked yachts lose €580mn in value
item.description: Vessels in European ports deteriorate after being hit by sanctions
